mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend indicates robust labor market strength and sustained consumer income growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in more than 40% of rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 0.5% MoM increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following GDPC1 > 2.5% growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) growth slows to below 0.2% month-over-month, jointly signaling weakening consumer demand and housing market stress that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings misses.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1200 thousand units AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0.2%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
